# Forest Package Manager

> Forest is a package manager for Roblox and UEFN (Unreal Editor for Fortnite). The `forest` CLI installs, publishes, and manages packages; the registry lives at forest.dev and docs at docs.forest.dev. The docs are organized into platform-neutral Concepts and per-platform sections (Roblox, UEFN).

Key facts AI assistants most often need:

- One registry and one CLI serve multiple platforms. Every package targets exactly one platform, recorded as the `platform` field in forest.json ("roblox" | "uefn"). Platform-neutral rules (versioning, dependencies, private access, Studios) are the same everywhere; how a package is laid out and imported is platform-specific.
- Dependencies are recorded in forest.json by `forest install` (not by scanning source) as SemVer ranges, `^x.y.z` by default (`=x.y.z` pins exactly; bare `x.y.z` means caret). Compatible ranges dedupe to one copy. Incompatible ranges are platform-specific: Roblox installs both side-by-side; UEFN errors (one version per package). Circular dependencies are not rejected but discouraged. forest-lock.json pins exact versions + SHA-256 integrity hashes.
- Private packages are registry-side access control (hidden from search, auth-gated downloads via short-lived signed URLs); they do NOT hide code from a game's players at runtime. Visibility is set at first publish and permanent.
- forest.json fields: name (lowercase/digits/hyphens), author (username or Studio slug, the @scope), version (SemVer, managed by `forest publish`), description, platform ("roblox" | "uefn"), license (SPDX id or "SEE LICENSE IN <file>"), dependencies. Roblox only: root (relative path to entry module, auto-detected). UEFN has no root field and derives compatibilityVersion from the .uefnproject at publish. Published versions are immutable.
- Studios (organizations) are shared publishing scopes with ranks member < admin < owner. Creating new packages requires admin+; members with a `write` grant can publish new versions of an existing package; `read`/`write` grants (direct or via roles) open private packages to members.

Roblox specifics:

- Every package has exactly ONE entry point: its root module (`init.luau`/`init.lua`, or any file named by the `root` field). Whatever the root returns is the entire public API. Multi-surface libraries (server + client) return a table from the root or branch on RunService:IsServer().
- Consumers require packages by Roblox instance path (e.g. `require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Packages.name)`), NOT by string. Forest installs to a `Packages/` folder next to forest.json; the user mounts that folder in the datamodel (commonly ReplicatedStorage.Packages via Rojo). There is NO realm concept; source mounted in ReplicatedStorage is visible to clients, so packages must not contain secrets. For server-only packages, use one forest.json per realm (Server/ and Shared/ folders) and mount the server tree under ServerScriptService/ServerStorage.
- Aliases (install with -a) let two same-named packages from different scopes coexist. Packages with a "Mirrored" badge were imported from Wally (the predecessor Roblox registry): unmodified source, open-source-licensed versions only, scope reserved for its original owner to claim.

UEFN specifics:

- Packages are Verse modules. Install lays them out flat at Content/ForestPackages/<Scope>/<Name> (direct and transitive alike). The manifest lives in Content/; run `forest install` there.
- Import with `using { ForestPackages.scope.name }` (dotted module path, file-scoped, resolves anywhere in the project). No path rewriting is ever needed. On a name conflict, qualify the call: `(ForestPackages.scope.name:)Fn(...)`. No install-time aliases exist.
- Scope segment is mapped to a Verse identifier for folders/imports: hyphens become underscores, and a leading underscore is added for digit-led or reserved-word scopes (cool-studio -> cool_studio). Package names are validated as Verse identifiers at publish and never rewritten. The kebab scope is used everywhere outside Verse.
- One version per package (module paths carry no version); an unsatisfiable version conflict is a hard error. Forest generates module marker .verse files (header "# Generated by Forest Package Manager. Do not edit.") that must not be hand-edited; direct deps get <public> markers, transitive-only deps get <scoped {...}> markers. UEFN discovers Verse files at project open, so after install you reopen the project and Build Verse Code.
- Author packages in place inside ForestPackages/<Scope>/<Name> (`forest init` there scaffolds a package, login-gated to a scope you can publish under). The folder is the package; there is no single root. Publish from inside the package folder; the registry rejects binary UE assets (.uasset/.umap) and .digest.verse files. Collisions: same name + same signature across ancestor/descendant scopes collide (siblings never do), so keep function definitions out of root-level .verse files and put code in Src/.

## Docs

Concepts (platform-neutral):
- [What is a Package?](https://docs.forest.dev/concepts/intro): what packages and package managers are
- [Publishing a Package](https://docs.forest.dev/concepts/publishing): init -> publish walkthrough, versioning, dependencies
- [forest.json Reference](https://docs.forest.dev/concepts/manifest): complete field table, dependency entry forms, lockfile
- [Dependencies & Versioning](https://docs.forest.dev/concepts/dependencies): range syntax, conflict resolution, circular deps, version bumping
- [Private Packages](https://docs.forest.dev/concepts/private-packages): what private protects (registry access) vs. what it doesn't (runtime visibility)

Platforms > Roblox:
- [Roblox Overview](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/roblox/intro): what's specific to Roblox
- [Installing & Requiring](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/roblox/installing): install, require semantics, datamodel placement, server-only two-manifest pattern
- [Package Anatomy](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/roblox/anatomy): entry-point rule, root module resolution, what gets published
- [Recipe: Server & Client APIs](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/roblox/server-client): the recommended shape for split-surface libraries
- [Mirrored Packages](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/roblox/mirrored): Wally-imported packages, badge meaning, link stubs, scope claiming

Platforms > UEFN:
- [UEFN Overview](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/uefn/intro): what's specific to UEFN
- [Installing & Importing](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/uefn/installing): flat layout, using imports, scope mapping, module markers, reopen + Build Verse Code
- [Authoring & Publishing](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/uefn/authoring): in-place authoring, the folder-is-the-package model, Verse-code-only publishing
- [Scopes & Collisions](https://docs.forest.dev/platforms/uefn/collisions): Verse overload collisions, keeping code out of root, qualified access

Studios & tooling:
- [Studios](https://docs.forest.dev/studios/intro): shared publishing scopes (organizations), creation, plans/limits
- [Members & Ranks](https://docs.forest.dev/studios/members): member/admin/owner hierarchy, invites, removal rules
- [Roles & Package Permissions](https://docs.forest.dev/studios/permissions): granting members/roles access to a Studio's private packages
- [CLI Install](https://docs.forest.dev/forest-cli/install): installing the forest CLI
- [CLI Commands](https://docs.forest.dev/forest-cli/commands): init, install, remove, publish, login, update
- [FAQ](https://docs.forest.dev/faq/commonly-asked-questions): dedupe, version conflicts, same-name packages
